trials.justification: All 5 ACs implemented with correct HTTP codes and error codes. AC5 idempotency is distinctly handled via short-circuit before findByEmail, so same-email never reaches the AC2 conflict path. No external validation library — uses a simple EMAIL_REGEX constant. 13 tests cover all 5 ACs with explicit names. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to specific test names. files_changed lists both handler and test file.

observations.text: AC5 idempotency correctly short-circuits after findById, skipping both findByEmail and updateEmail — cleanly distinct from AC2
Guard ordering is deliberate: format → existence → idempotency → conflict → update, with an extra test verifying ordering
AC3 uses a simple EMAIL_REGEX constant, no external library
13 tests with 7 parametrised cases for AC3 format validation shows thorough coverage
Handler factory pattern with injected repository makes testing clean without mocking frameworks
Assumption about findByEmail is explicitly noted — since AC5 short-circuits before findByEmail, the same-user ownership check on the conflict path is correctly unnecessary

It asked 'Do you want me to proceed?' instead of delivering. No acceptance_criteria_coverage mapping was provided, no files_changed list was given, and no test results were shown. The described design sounds correct (guard order includes same-email idempotency distinct from conflict check, no external library), but without actual code, test output, or structured metadata, only partial credit is warranted.
